title: vicia abbreviata subsp. montenegrina
intro: Vicia abbreviata subsp. montenegrina, also known as Montenegrin vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Montenegrin vetch include relieving symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and allergies, as well as soothing gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and cramps. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions made from its leaves, stems, or roots, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
